title: The Brave Heart, Underneath
scripture_text: Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened.
reflection: The hardest thing a parent does is to not be the answer anymore. The letting go is part of the love. (The fourth hearing of this day.)
body:
The bags are packed. The room is half-empty. The child you have known for eighteen, twenty-two, twenty-eight years is leaving, and the house is going to be a different shape.

You have done the work. You have taught what you know. You have shown up for the bedtime and the breakfast and the soccer game and the late-night conversation. They are leaving because you did your job.

It will not feel like a victory. It will feel like an amputation. Some nights it will feel like both at once.

The Lord, who let His own Son go to a cross, knows the math of this. He did not love His Son less for letting Him go. He loved Him in the letting go.

Let them go. They will come back. Or they will not. Either way, the love is already finished. It is already complete.

The fear is not a sign that something is wrong. The fear is a sign that you are doing something brave. The people of the Bible, when they heard the call of God, were mostly afraid. Moses said, "I am slow of speech." Gideon said, "I am the least in my father's house." Jeremiah said, "I am a child." David was a shepherd. The fear is the calling's costume. You put it on when you step into the thing. You take it off later, on the other side.

The Lord does not ask you to be unafraid. He asks you to be brave. Brave is being afraid and stepping anyway. The Lord is the step. The Lord is the road. The Lord is the hand at your back.

The fear does not have to leave before the step. The step and the fear can walk together.

The fear is not a sign that something is wrong. The fear is a sign that you are doing something brave. The people of the Bible, when they heard the call of God, were mostly afraid. Moses said, "I am slow of speech." Gideon said, "I am the least in my father's house." Jeremiah said, "I am a child." David was a shepherd. The fear is the calling's costume. You put it on when you step into the thing. You take it off later, on the other side.

The Lord does not ask you to be unafraid. He asks you to be brave. Brave is being afraid and stepping anyway. The Lord is the step. The Lord is the road. The Lord is the hand at your back.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

The fear does not have to leave before the step. The step and the fear can walk together.

This is the fourth time. You have gone deeper now than you went the first time. The well is deeper. The water is still. The Lord is at the bottom. Keep going.
prayer: Lord, I am letting go. Hold them for me. And hold me. Amen.
